channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Cowboys make clutch defensive plays to stymie Bucs, pull off win
item.description: The Dallas Cowboys' secondary came up clutch against the Atlanta Falcons on Sunday with two key turnovers in the fourth quarter. Dallas won the game, 26-24.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bucs' Cody Mauch adjusting to and enjoying Tampa Bay's warm winters
item.description: Bucs' offensive lineman Cody Mauch is in his second year in the NFL, which means he is spending another Christmas in paradise.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Facing hometown Canucks 'a bit surreal' for Sharks rookie Macklin Celebrini
item.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia (AP) — Macklin Celebrini grew up dreaming about playing NHL hockey at Rogers Arena. On Monday night, that dream will become a reality. “Skating here when I was younger, I kind of tried to put myself in an NHL player’s situation or shoes, and pretend to be them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Instant analysis of 49ers’ 29-17 loss to Dolphins amid playoff elimination
item.description: The 49ers (6-9) were officially eliminated from playoff contention Sunday, even before kickoff of their 29-17 defeat to the Miami Dolphins (7-8).
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: Tiger's son makes the ace. Bernhard Langer makes the winning putt in playoff over Woods at PNC
item.description: Tiger Woods never had so much fun losing in a playoff. For the 67-year-old Bernhard Langer, it never gets old winning the PNC Championship with his son.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jalen Hurts leaves with a concussion as the Eagles' winning streak ends at 10
item.description: LANDOVER, Md. (AP) — Jalen Hurts was concussed minutes into the game and ruled out after evaluation, Kenny Pickett played like a backup quarterback and the Philadelphia Eagles' winning streak is over.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Charlie Woods, 15, makes first ever hole-in-one at PNC Championship
item.description: Charlie Woods, 15, made his first career hole-in-one while competing alongside his dad in the PNC Championship in Florida on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College Football Playoffs looks for good matchups after snoozy first round of blowouts
item.description: For those who dozed through it, or flipped to more compelling fare in the NFL, here's a brief recap of the rollout of the much-anticipated College Football Playoff. Average final score: Winners 36, Losers 17.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Day 3: Winter Classic Basketball
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Palisade High School was buzzing on Day 3 of the Winter Classic as the Fruita Wildcats faced off against the Palmer Ridge Bears. Fruita entered the game riding high after a recent win against Ponderosa, while Palmer Ridge looked to snap a three-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oakland legend, Hall of Famer Rickey Henderson has died at the age of 65
item.description: (KRON) – Rickey Henderson, one of the greatest baseball players to ever live, died at age 65. Henderson is Major League Baseball’s all-time stolen base leader and is considered by many to be the greatest player in A’s franchise history.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Senate passes RFK Stadium land bill, giving the Washington Commanders a major off-the-field win
item.description: The path for the NFL's Washington Commanders to return to the nation’s capital is clear after an on-again, off-again saga in Congress ended early Saturday with a postmidnight reprieve.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker said it is “debatable” whether transgender women will “always” have a physical advantage over cisgender, or non-transgender, women.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmaker wants Chicago women's sports teams to have an even playing field
item.description: State Rep. Eva-Dina Delgado (D-Chicago) has introduced legislation requiring equity in public stadium funding for men’s and women’s sports. Illinois lawmakers began hearings this week on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Government funding deal opens door to move Washington Commanders stadium to DC
item.description: The Washington Commanders could soon be kicking off in the heart of the nation’s capital. The government funding deal unveiled by congressional negotiators Tuesday paved the way for moving the Commanders' stadium from Maryland to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa native named starting QB for Atlanta Falcons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Atlanta Falcons are turning to rookie Michael Penix Jr. as their starting quarterback and benching veteran Kirk Cousins as they fight for their first playoff berth since 2017. Coach Raheem Morris announced the decision in a statement Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Donato caps Blackhawks comeback, Blackhawks top Capitals 3-2
item.description: Ryan Donato scored his team-leading 11th goal with 2:18 left in regulation to cap Chicago's three-goal, third-period rally and lift the Blackhawks to a 3-2 win over the Washington Capitals on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 84-year-old continues lawn bowling despite vision loss
item.description: On most days, you can find Ralph Trinity rolling with the Oaks North Lawn Bowling Club in Rancho Bernardo.
item.dc:creator: Troy Hirsch, Fox 5

item.title: BYU excited to face Heisman winner Travis Hunter
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - As BYU continues preparation for the Alamo Bowl December 28th against Colorado, they are looking forward to facing off against Heisman Trophy Winner Travis Hunter. Hunter is truly a one-of-a-kind player.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
